backup_api_paths: / (filer root, Accept: application/json -> JSON dir listing with Path/Entries/ShouldDisplayLoadMore)
/buckets/ (filer view of S3 bucket roots when filer is colocated with S3 gateway)
/ (S3 endpoint :8333 GET / -> ListAllMyBucketsResult XML, anon ListBuckets when auth off)
/{bucket}/?list-type=2 (S3 ListObjectsV2 -> ListBucketResult XML, object key names)
ai_scope_discriminator: Confirm AI/ML payload from NAMES ONLY. (1) S3 path: GET :8333/ returns ListAllMyBucketsResult XML; read <Bucket><Name> values for ML-signal buckets: models, model-registry, checkpoints, ckpt, datasets, training-data, train, artifacts, mlflow, mlflow-artifacts, dvc, lakefs, weights, hf-cache, huggingface, embeddings, vectors, tensorboard, sagemaker, kubeflow, pipelines, feast, feature-store. (2) Filer path: GET :8888/ with Accept: application/json returns {Entries:[{FullPath,Mime,...}]} -- read FullPath/name fields for the same tokens plus model file extensions in entry names (.safetensors, .ckpt, .pt, .pth, .gguf, .onnx, .bin, .pkl, .npz, .parquet, .tfrecord) and MLflow layout markers (mlflow/, artifacts/<run-id>/, meta.yaml, MLmodel). (3) DVC/lakeFS-on-SeaweedFS: bucket/dir names like .dvc, dvc-cache, lakefs, or repo-shaped prefixes. Listing names and MIME types is metadata read; fetching any object body (GET /bucket/key with a real key, GET filer file path) is exfiltration -- do not.
fingerprint_markers: HTTP response header: Server: SeaweedFS <maxVolumeSize> <version> (e.g. 'Server: SeaweedFS 30GB 3.96') -- vendor-unique, version embedded
Filer JSON dir listing keys: "Path", "Entries", "Limit", "LastFileName", "ShouldDisplayLoadMore" (SeaweedFS-specific filer response shape, not standard S3)
Filer HTML index page title/listing rendered by the filer UI on :8888
S3 ListAllMyBucketsResult XML emitted with empty namespace <ListAllMyBucketsResult xmlns=''> (SeaweedFS quirk, distinct from AWS/MinIO which set xmlns="http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/")
Master :9333 /dir/status and /cluster/status JSON exposing Topology/DataCenters/volume layout
weed binary process identity; ports 8333(S3)/8888(filer)/9333(master)/8080(volume) co-resident on one host
fp_guards: Require the literal 'SeaweedFS' token in the Server header -- generic S3/MinIO never emit it (MinIO sends 'Server: MinIO', AWS sends 'Server: AmazonS3'). The Server string alone separates this vendor; a bare :8333/:9000 port match does NOT.

Do not match on ListBucketResult / ListAllMyBucketsResult XML alone -- that schema is shared by every S3-compatible store. Disambiguate with the empty xmlns='' quirk AND/OR the SeaweedFS Server header.

Filer listing must show the SeaweedFS key set (ShouldDisplayLoadMore + LastFileName + Entries together). Generic directory-index pages (nginx autoindex, Apache mod_autoindex, h5ai, Caddy) lack these JSON keys -- exclude them.
Port 9333 + /dir/status Topology JSON is master-specific; do not confuse with Consul/etcd cluster-status endpoints on nearby ports.

Exclude honeypot/echo hosts that reflect 'SeaweedFS' in a title but return no valid filer JSON or no ListAllMyBucketsResult on the matching port (catch-all-negative guard): a real instance answers the filer JSON shape on :8888 and/or the XML on :8333.

cves.desc: Path traversal in the S3 and Iceberg REST gateways (SkipClean(true) keeps '..' segments; captured path vars unvalidated) allows cross-bucket read/write. Unauthenticated when auth is off; authorization-confusion / tenant-isolation break when auth is on. Affects < 4.30; fixed in 4.30. GHSA-w62w-66v9-vvgv, High, published 2026-06-10.
SQL injection in the SeaweedFS filer when configured with a SQL metadata backend (e.g. MySQL filer store) via crafted path/filename inputs. Moderate; patched.

pivot_paths: GET :8333/ -> ListAllMyBucketsResult: enumerate bucket NAMES only (no object GET)
GET :8888/?pretty=y or Accept: application/json -> filer root Entries: read directory/file NAMES + MIME, page with ?limit=&lastFileName= (names only)
GET :8333/{bucket}/?list-type=2 -> ListBucketResult: read object KEY names for a single named bucket (no body fetch)
GET :9333/dir/status and :9333/cluster/status -> read cluster Topology / volume layout (capacity, DC/rack names) for operator attribution
GET :8080/status on a volume server -> read VolumeInfos (volume IDs, sizes) without touching file data
Cross-reference TLS cert CN / favicon across the co-resident 8333/8888/9333/8080 set to attribute the operator (passive)
restraint_note: Names-and-metadata only. Bucket names, object KEY names, filer directory/file names, MIME types, and master/volume status JSON are the finding -- read them. Do NOT download any object body (no GET /bucket/key on a real key, no GET of a filer file path), do NOT PUT/DELETE/copy, do NOT call s3.configure or any bucket-policy/ACL change, do NOT exercise the CVE-2026-54917 traversal to cross into another bucket. A reachable anon ListBuckets / filer listing is 'surface open, listing exercised'; object contents stay untouched. Sample a payload only if severity confirmation is unavoidable, minimally, and log it.
